behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is parser-to-handler handoff, not just raw JSON.parse. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: this could be Unit if one function both parses and handles; however behavior text says "proceed through normal hook input handling," which implies module interaction. Rejected higher levels because no real process/OS/network and no model output dependency are required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test through the real parser + hook input handler + fake trace sink with a valid payload containing code fences, backticks, and $vars; assert parse success, normal handler result, and no parse-failure trace.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is parser error being transformed into a diagnostic trace payload (cross-module contract). REAL-INFRA: not required if parser and trace formatter run in-process. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: could be Unit if trace formatting is inside parser module; behavior explicitly requires emitted trace record with diagnostic context, which is commonly an integration boundary. Rejected System because no requirement for real subprocess/stdin in this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Feed malformed JSON into the in-process parse entrypoint wired to the real trace-record builder; assert trace type `json_parse_failed` and presence of contextual fields (e.g., offset/snippet/error message) that identify malformed region.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is policy mapping logic on a known parse-failure input; no required module handoff is stated beyond local policy. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: might be Integration if telemetry intent marking requires another module contract; text says "local error-policy path," which disqualifies needing broader wiring as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy function with a parse-failure object and assert returned handled outcome (no exception) plus telemetry-intent flag/state set as expected.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit real process execution + stdin + exit behavior; in-process fakes can miss crash/exit/pipe ordering issues. MOCK-HIDE: mocking subprocess/stdin could hide real termination behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: one might try Integration with direct function calls, but behavior text explicitly requires real process execution, which disqualifies lower levels.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook executable as a subprocess, pipe malformed stdin, wait for exit, assert non-crash behavior (controlled exit) and verify expected parse-failure trace signal is emitted before process termination.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior names real invocation path starting from stdin, so subprocess/OS I/O boundary is part of failure mode. MOCK-HIDE: replacing stdin/process with in-memory calls can miss framing/stream handling faults. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: could be Integration if "real" were interpreted loosely, but explicit `stdin -> parser -> trace sink` real invocation wording keeps System as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke hook via its real entrypoint with malformed stdin payload and assert the produced trace artifact in the configured sink/output channel matches expected parse-failure signal and structure.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: distinction depends on classification across parser outcome + tracing/error categorization, typically across module boundaries. REAL-INFRA: not inherently required; can be done in-process with fixture payloads. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: could be Unit if a single classifier function decides both cases; behavior framing as regression attribution across valid-vs-malformed payload handling supports Integration minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Run paired in-process fixtures through the real parsing/handling pipeline: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings, (b) malformed JSON. Assert only (b) yields `json_parse_failed`, while (a) follows normal path, proving markdown content alone is not blamed.
